title: penstemon laevigatus var. deamii
intro: Penstemon laevigatus var. deamii, also known as Deam's beardtongue, is a perennial plant that belongs to the plantain family (Plantaginaceae) and is native to the prairies and open woods of the Midwestern United States. Historically, the plant was used in folk medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic effects. The health benefits of Deam's beardtongue include treating wounds, fever, and respiratory issues, as well as providing relief from digestive problems. Traditionally, the plant's roots and leaves are prepared in herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures to harness its medicinal properties.
